title: Hanwha Philly Shipyard and TOTE Services Selected to Build New MDA Missile Range Instrumentation Vessels
summary: TOTE Services and Hanwha Philly Shipyard have been selected by the U.S. Missile Defense Agency (MDA) to build new Missile Range Instrumentation Vessels. The first vessel in this program will be named 'Golden Defender,' providing critical capabilities for missile defense testing and data acquisition.

title: US Air Force makes its first wingman drone missile launch
summary: In a US first, the US Air Force has successfully fired a missile from a Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA) Loyal Wingman. Conducted in collaboration with Anduril Industries, the YFQ-44A drone launched an AIM-120 missile at a digital target. Continue Reading Category: Military , Engineering Tags: US Air Force , …

title: World-first airdrop of an autonomous drone boat into rough seas
summary: In a world first, the Royal Air Force airdropped an autonomous drone boat from a military transport aircraft. Part of the Royal Navy's Project Beehive, a Kraken Technologies K3 SCOUT vehicle was parachuted from an Airbus A400M cargo plane over the North Sea. title: The FBI reportedly won’t investigate ICE anymore
summary: According to the New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to The Times. The reported change in guidance follows renewed scrutiny of violence by ICE agents who have killed two people in …

title: Inside Zcash's new node that targets Visa-scale privacy at 50,000 transactions per second
source: CoinDesk: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Crypto News and Price Data
summary: Zcash has introduced a new node designed to achieve Visa-scale transaction privacy, aiming for a throughput of 50,000 transactions per second. This development represents a significant technical advancement for Zcash, pushing the boundaries of privacy-preserving blockchain technology to handle high volumes of transactions while maintaining user anonymity.

title: Bitcoin’s quantum problem gets a recovery tool, but not for Satoshi’s 1.1 million coins
source: CoinDesk: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Crypto News and Price Data
summary: A new recovery tool has been developed to address Bitcoin's theoretical vulnerability to quantum computing attacks, offering a method to secure funds against future quantum threats. However, this tool is not applicable to Satoshi Nakamoto's estimated 1.1 million Bitcoin, which remain potentially exposed due to their specific cryptographic characteristics.

title: US agencies miss GENIUS Act deadline for final stablecoin rules
summary: U.S. regulatory agencies have missed the one-year deadline set by the GENIUS Act for finalizing stablecoin regulations. Instead of issuing final rules, regulators have published 10 proposed rules, indicating a delay in establishing a definitive regulatory framework for stablecoins. This marks a significant setback in providing clarity for the crypto market.

title: Tether's USDT hits 2-year countdown threatening its position on U.S. crypto platforms
source: CoinDesk: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Crypto News and Price Data
summary: Tether's USDT stablecoin is reportedly facing a two-year countdown that could jeopardize its continued availability on U.S. crypto platforms. This situation likely stems from impending regulatory changes or compliance requirements that Tether may struggle to meet within the specified timeframe, potentially impacting its market position in the U.S.

title: Upbit parent Dunamu faces South Korean regulator sanctions process: Report
summary: Dunamu, the parent company of South Korean crypto exchange Upbit, is reportedly facing a sanctions process from South Korean regulators. The situation is complicated by the Virtual Asset User Protection Act, which lacks explicit provisions for penalties related to hacking or similar incidents, creating uncertainty regarding the scope of potential sanctions.
